account_details.description: Master table containing comprehensive information about all bank accounts including balances, status, and branch details
account_details.business_purpose: Serves as the central repository for account information, tracking current balances, account types, and operational status for all customer accounts within the bank

account_name.description: Name of the account holder or account title

branch_code.description: Code of the branch where account is held

branch_name.description: Name of the branch where account is held

opening_date.description: Date when the account was opened

closing_date.description: Date when the account was closed (if applicable)

minimum_balance.description: Minimum balance required for this account

actual_balance.description: Actual account balance including pending transactions

created_at.description: When the account record was created

updated_at.description: When the account record was last updated

referenced_by.description: One account can have multiple holders (joint accounts)
One account can be payer in multiple transfers
One account can be payee in multiple transfers
One account has multiple transaction entries
One account can have multiple limit types
One account can have multiple beneficiaries
One account can be beneficiary for multiple payers

common_joins.purpose: Get account ownership information
common_joins.sql_example: JOIN account_holders ah ON ad.account_id = ah.account_id

common_joins.purpose: Get customer information for account holders
common_joins.sql_example: JOIN account_holders ah ON ad.account_id = ah.account_id JOIN customers c ON ah.customer_id = c.customer_id

common_joins.purpose: Get transfers where account is payer or payee
common_joins.sql_example: JOIN payment_instructions pi ON (ad.account_id = pi.payer_account_id OR ad.account_id = pi.payee_account_id)

customers.description: Customer master data containing personal information, contact details, and KYC status for all bank customers
customers.business_purpose: Maintains complete customer profiles including identity verification status, contact information, and demographic data required for banking operations and compliance

created_at.description: When the customer record was created

updated_at.description: When the customer record was last updated

referenced_by.description: One customer can hold multiple accounts

common_joins.purpose: Get customer's account relationships
common_joins.sql_example: JOIN account_holders ah ON c.customer_id = ah.customer_id

common_joins.purpose: Get all accounts owned by customer
common_joins.sql_example: JOIN account_holders ah ON c.customer_id = ah.customer_id JOIN account_details ad ON ah.account_id = ad.account_id

common_joins.join_condition: Via account_holders and account_details to get customer's transfers
common_joins.purpose: Get all transfers involving customer's accounts
common_joins.sql_example: JOIN account_holders ah ON c.customer_id = ah.customer_id JOIN payment_instructions pi ON (ah.account_id = pi.payer_account_id OR ah.account_id = pi.payee_account_id)

account_holders.description: Junction table establishing relationships between customers and accounts, supporting multiple ownership types including joint accounts and authorized signatories
account_holders.business_purpose: Manages complex account ownership structures by linking customers to accounts with specific relationship types, enabling joint accounts and authorized signatory arrangements
common_queries: find all accounts for a customer

id.description: Auto-increment primary key for the relationship record

relationship_type.description: Type of relationship between customer and account

effective_date.description: Date when the relationship became effective

end_date.description: Date when the relationship ended (if applicable)

created_at.description: When the relationship record was created

foreign_keys.description: Links to the account being held
Links to the customer holding the account

common_joins.purpose: Get account information for the relationship
common_joins.sql_example: JOIN account_details ad ON ah.account_id = ad.account_id

common_joins.purpose: Get customer information for the account holder
common_joins.sql_example: JOIN customers c ON ah.customer_id = c.customer_id

common_joins.purpose: Complete customer-account relationship with full details
common_joins.sql_example: JOIN account_details ad ON ah.account_id = ad.account_id JOIN customers c ON ah.customer_id = c.customer_id

payment_instructions.description: Central transaction table containing all internal transfer requests, amounts, scheduling information, and processing status from initiation to completion
payment_instructions.business_purpose: Tracks the complete lifecycle of internal money transfers including immediate, scheduled, and recurring payments with full audit trail and maker-checker approval workflow

payment_purpose.description: Purpose or reason for the payment

scheduled_date.description: When the transfer should be executed

execution_date.description: When the transfer was actually executed

narrative.description: Additional description or notes for the transfer

instruction_status.description: Current status of the payment instruction

priority.description: Processing priority level of the transfer

created_by.description: User who created the payment instruction

authorized_by.description: User who authorized the payment instruction

updated_at.description: When the instruction was last updated

referenced_by.description: Payment instruction can have one recurring schedule
One payment instruction generates multiple transaction entries
Payment instruction can have multiple error entries

common_joins.purpose: Get payer account information
common_joins.sql_example: JOIN account_details payer ON pi.payer_account_id = payer.account_id

common_joins.purpose: Get payee account information
common_joins.sql_example: JOIN account_details payee ON pi.payee_account_id = payee.account_id

common_joins.purpose: Get recurring payment schedule details
common_joins.sql_example: LEFT JOIN recurrence_instructions ri ON pi.instruction_id = ri.instruction_id

common_joins.purpose: Get detailed transaction entries for the payment
common_joins.sql_example: JOIN transactions t ON pi.instruction_id = t.instruction_id

common_joins.join_condition: Via account_details and account_holders to get customer information
common_joins.purpose: Get payer and payee customer details
common_joins.sql_example: JOIN account_details payer ON pi.payer_account_id = payer.account_id JOIN account_holders ah ON payer.account_id = ah.account_id JOIN customers c ON ah.customer_id = c.customer_id

recurrence_instructions.description: Configuration table for recurring payment patterns including frequency, scheduling rules, and execution tracking for automated repeated transfers
recurrence_instructions.business_purpose: Enables automated recurring payments by defining complex scheduling patterns, tracking execution history, and managing the lifecycle of repetitive transfer instructions

recurrence_id.description: Unique identifier for the recurrence pattern

instruction_id.description: Payment instruction that this recurrence applies to

day_of_week.description: Day of week for weekly recurrence

day_of_month.description: Day of month for monthly recurrence (1-31 or negative for end of month)

occurrences_completed.description: Number of executions completed so far

next_execution_date.description: Date of the next scheduled execution

updated_at.description: When the recurrence was last updated

foreign_keys.description: Links to the base payment instruction that repeats

common_joins.purpose: Get the base payment instruction details
common_joins.sql_example: JOIN payment_instructions pi ON ri.instruction_id = pi.instruction_id

common_joins.join_condition: Via payment_instructions to get payer and payee account details
common_joins.purpose: Get account information for recurring payments
common_joins.sql_example: JOIN payment_instructions pi ON ri.instruction_id = pi.instruction_id JOIN account_details payer ON pi.payer_account_id = payer.account_id

common_joins.purpose: Get customer information for recurring payment participants
common_joins.sql_example: JOIN payment_instructions pi ON ri.instruction_id = pi.instruction_id JOIN account_details ad ON pi.payer_account_id = ad.account_id JOIN account_holders ah ON ad.account_id = ah.account_id JOIN customers c ON ah.customer_id = c.customer_id

transactions.description: Detailed transaction ledger recording individual debit and credit entries for all processed transfers with before/after balance tracking
transactions.business_purpose: Maintains complete double-entry transaction records for audit compliance, balance reconciliation, and detailed transaction history for all account movements

transaction_id.description: Unique identifier for the transaction entry

instruction_id.description: Payment instruction that generated this transaction

contra_account_id.description: The other account involved in the transfer

posting_date.description: Date when the transaction was posted

processed_by.description: User or system that processed the transaction

foreign_keys.description: Links to the payment instruction that generated this transaction
Account affected by this transaction entry
The other account involved in the transfer

referenced_by.description: Transaction can have multiple error entries

common_joins.purpose: Get payment instruction details for the transaction
common_joins.sql_example: JOIN payment_instructions pi ON t.instruction_id = pi.instruction_id

common_joins.purpose: Get account information for the transaction
common_joins.sql_example: JOIN account_details ad ON t.account_id = ad.account_id

common_joins.purpose: Get contra account information
common_joins.sql_example: JOIN account_details contra ON t.contra_account_id = contra.account_id

common_joins.join_condition: Via account_details and account_holders to get customer information
common_joins.purpose: Get customer information for transaction participants
common_joins.sql_example: JOIN account_details ad ON t.account_id = ad.account_id JOIN account_holders ah ON ad.account_id = ah.account_id JOIN customers c ON ah.customer_id = c.customer_id

account_limits.description: Account-specific transaction limits and controls defining maximum amounts for daily, monthly, and per-transaction transfer restrictions
account_limits.business_purpose: Enforces risk management and regulatory compliance by setting and tracking transaction limits per account, preventing unauthorized large transfers and managing exposure

limit_id.description: Auto-increment primary key for the limit record

account_id.description: Account that the limit applies to

limit_amount.description: Maximum amount for this limit type

effective_date.description: Date when the limit becomes effective

is_active.description: Whether the limit is currently active

updated_at.description: When the limit was last updated

foreign_keys.description: Account that the limit applies to

common_joins.purpose: Get account information for the limits
common_joins.sql_example: JOIN account_details ad ON al.account_id = ad.account_id

common_joins.join_condition: Via account_details and account_holders to get customer information
common_joins.purpose: Get customer information for account limits
common_joins.sql_example: JOIN account_details ad ON al.account_id = ad.account_id JOIN account_holders ah ON ad.account_id = ah.account_id JOIN customers c ON ah.customer_id = c.customer_id

common_joins.join_condition: Via account_details to check limits against transfers
common_joins.purpose: Compare transfer amounts against account limits
common_joins.sql_example: JOIN account_details ad ON al.account_id = ad.account_id JOIN payment_instructions pi ON (ad.account_id = pi.payer_account_id OR ad.account_id = pi.payee_account_id)

beneficiaries.description: Pre-approved payee directory containing validated recipient accounts for streamlined and secure repeat transfers
beneficiaries.business_purpose: Enhances security and user experience by maintaining a list of verified transfer recipients, reducing fraud risk and simplifying repeat payment processes

beneficiary_id.description: Unique identifier for the beneficiary relationship

payer_account_id.description: Account that can send money to this beneficiary

approval_required.description: Whether beneficiary requires approval for activation

approved_by.description: User who approved the beneficiary (if applicable)

approval_date.description: Date when the beneficiary was approved

last_used_date.description: Date when this beneficiary was last used for a transfer

usage_count.description: Number of times this beneficiary has been used

updated_at.description: When the beneficiary was last updated

foreign_keys.description: Account that can send money to this beneficiary

common_joins.purpose: Get payer account information
common_joins.sql_example: JOIN account_details payer ON b.payer_account_id = payer.account_id

common_joins.purpose: Get beneficiary account information
common_joins.sql_example: JOIN account_details payee ON b.payee_account_id = payee.account_id

common_joins.join_condition: Via account_details and account_holders to get customer information
common_joins.purpose: Get customer information for payer and payee
common_joins.sql_example: JOIN account_details payer ON b.payer_account_id = payer.account_id JOIN account_holders ah ON payer.account_id = ah.account_id JOIN customers c ON ah.customer_id = c.customer_id

common_joins.purpose: Get transfer history to this beneficiary
common_joins.sql_example: JOIN payment_instructions pi ON (b.payer_account_id = pi.payer_account_id AND b.payee_account_id = pi.payee_account_id)

audit_trail.description: Comprehensive audit log capturing all system changes, user actions, and data modifications with detailed before/after values for compliance tracking
audit_trail.business_purpose: Ensures regulatory compliance and security by maintaining immutable records of all system activities, enabling investigation, compliance reporting, and change tracking

audit_id.description: Auto-increment primary key for the audit record

table_name.description: Name of the table that was modified

record_id.description: ID of the specific record that was modified

old_values.description: Previous values before the change (for UPDATE and DELETE)

new_values.description: New values after the change (for INSERT and UPDATE)

changed_fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields that were changed

session_id.description: Session identifier for the user action

ip_address.description: IP address from which the change was made

user_agent.description: User agent string of the client making the change

common_joins.purpose: Get audit history for specific payment instructions
common_joins.sql_example: JOIN payment_instructions pi ON (at.table_name = 'payment_instructions' AND at.record_id = pi.instruction_id)

common_joins.purpose: Get audit history for account changes
common_joins.sql_example: JOIN account_details ad ON (at.table_name = 'account_details' AND at.record_id = ad.account_id)

common_joins.purpose: Get audit history for customer changes
common_joins.sql_example: JOIN customers c ON (at.table_name = 'customers' AND at.record_id = c.customer_id)

error_logs.description: System error tracking table recording technical failures, business rule violations, and processing errors with resolution status
error_logs.business_purpose: Supports system stability and troubleshooting by capturing detailed error information, tracking resolution progress, and identifying recurring issues for system improvement

error_id.description: Auto-increment primary key for the error record

instruction_id.description: Payment instruction that caused the error (if applicable)

transaction_id.description: Transaction that caused the error (if applicable)

error_message.description: Detailed error message describing the issue

module_name.description: System module where the error occurred

function_name.description: Specific function where the error occurred

stack_trace.description: Technical stack trace for debugging purposes

resolved.description: Whether the error has been resolved

resolved_at.description: Date and time when the error was resolved

resolution_notes.description: Notes about how the error was resolved

foreign_keys.description: Payment instruction that caused the error (if applicable)
Transaction that caused the error (if applicable)

common_joins.purpose: Get payment instruction details for the error
common_joins.sql_example: LEFT JOIN payment_instructions pi ON el.instruction_id = pi.instruction_id

common_joins.purpose: Get transaction details for the error
common_joins.sql_example: LEFT JOIN transactions t ON el.transaction_id = t.transaction_id

common_joins.join_condition: Via payment_instructions to get account information related to errors
common_joins.purpose: Get account information for payment-related errors
common_joins.sql_example: LEFT JOIN payment_instructions pi ON el.instruction_id = pi.instruction_id LEFT JOIN account_details ad ON pi.payer_account_id = ad.account_id

system_parameters.description: System configuration settings and business rules including transfer limits, processing times, and operational parameters
system_parameters.business_purpose: Centralizes system configuration management, enabling dynamic adjustment of business rules, limits, and operational parameters without code changes

parameter_id.description: Auto-increment primary key for the parameter

parameter_name.description: Unique name of the system parameter

parameter_value.description: Value of the parameter (stored as text)

parameter_type.description: Data type of the parameter value

is_encrypted.description: Whether the parameter value is encrypted

updated_at.description: When the parameter was last updated

updated_by.description: User who last updated the parameter

common_joins.join_condition: Used for applying business rules and limits validation
common_joins.purpose: Apply system parameters to validate payment instructions
common_joins.sql_example: -- Parameters are typically used in WHERE clauses for validation rather than JOINs

common_joins.join_condition: Used for comparing account limits against system-wide limits
common_joins.purpose: Compare account-specific limits with system parameters
common_joins.sql_example: -- System parameters provide default or maximum values for comparison

holiday_calendar.description: Banking holiday and non-processing day calendar affecting transfer scheduling and same-day processing availability
holiday_calendar.business_purpose: Ensures accurate transfer scheduling by identifying non-business days when certain processing operations are unavailable, supporting proper settlement timing

holiday_id.description: Auto-increment primary key for the holiday record

created_at.description: When the holiday record was created

common_joins.join_condition: Used to validate scheduled_date against holidays
common_joins.purpose: Ensure transfers are not scheduled on holidays
common_joins.sql_example: LEFT JOIN holiday_calendar hc ON DATE(pi.scheduled_date) = hc.holiday_date

common_joins.join_condition: Used to calculate next_execution_date avoiding holidays
common_joins.purpose: Skip holidays when calculating recurring payment dates
common_joins.sql_example: LEFT JOIN holiday_calendar hc ON ri.next_execution_date = hc.holiday_date

common_joins.join_condition: Used to validate value_date and posting_date
common_joins.purpose: Ensure transactions are not posted on holidays
common_joins.sql_example: LEFT JOIN holiday_calendar hc ON t.value_date = hc.holiday_date
